name: Pointer utilities
description: Dictates styling and available actions for visible cursors and pointer/touch actions.

families.name: cursor
families.description: Sets the visual display of the cursor icon.
guidance: Use the appropriate contextual cursor when it provides meaningful information to the end user. 'Pointer' for when the cursor is above interactive (clickable) elements, 'not-allowed' when the element is in a disabled state, 'grab' when an element is draggable, etc. Hyper specific cursors like 'vertical-text' or 'copy' are not necessary - use 'pointer' for a copy button and the default cursor for an input element or when hovering over text content instead.

families.name: pointer-events
families.description: Determines the interactivy level of content when hovered over with a mouse.
guidance: Use 'none' if you need an overlay or icon to not block element interactivity behind it.

families.name: user-select
families.description: Controls whether users can select text within an element.
guidance: Use 'none' on interactive items with text within them to prevent touch interaction from triggering text selection if pressed for too long. Use 'all' for bodies of copy (like a code block) that a user would conceivably want to select and copy in a full chunk.

families.name: touch-action
families.description: Determines how the browser manages default touchscreen gestures (like panning, scrolling, and zooming) over a specific element
guidance: Be wary of overusing 'none' or applying it on elements like <body> as it will disable zoom ability for users who may need it for accessibility reasons.
